pending.body: De-duplicate bead-graph serialization between src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs and src/test_support/fixtures.rs. The new file redefines `SerializedScenarioIssue` (e2e_fixtures.rs:873-899), `SerializedScenarioDependency` (e2e_fixtures.rs:901-911), their `From<&BeadGraphIssue>`/`From<&BeadGraphDependency>` impls (e2e_fixtures.rs:913-953), and `serialize_acceptance_criteria` (e2e_fixtures.rs:857-871) verbatim from fixtures.rs (fixtures.rs:66-79 and 211-292). Both paths currently produce identical JSON, but they are on separate evolution paths: `BeadGraphFixtureBuilder::write_into` (fixtures.rs) writes the initial .beads/issues.jsonl using SerializedBeadGraphIssue, while `ScenarioState::persist` (e2e_fixtures.rs:561-578) rewrites it after every create/close/dep-add using SerializedScenarioIssue. If a field is ever added to the canonical serializer in fixtures.rs (a realistic change — acceptance_criteria already has custom formatting logic), the mutation path will silently strip it, breaking tests in hard-to-debug ways. Fix by exposing a shared helper from fixtures.rs — either `pub(crate) fn write_bead_graph_issues(path: &Path, issues: &[BeadGraphIssue]) -> AppResult<()>` or `pub(crate)` visibility on the Serialized* structs — and calling it from ScenarioState::persist. This keeps a single source of truth for the on-disk shape.
